# pfsense_firewall_nat_one_to_one (Resource)

[Firewall NAT 1:1](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/nat/1-1.html). Maps an external IP address to an internal IP address (bidirectional NAT).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "pfsense_firewall_nat_one_to_one" "webserver" {
  interface           = "wan"
  external            = "203.0.113.10"
  ipprotocol          = "inet"
  source_address      = "any"
  destination_address = "192.168.1.10"
  description         = "Web server 1:1 NAT"
}

resource "pfsense_firewall_nat_one_to_one" "mailserver" {
  interface           = "wan"
  external            = "203.0.113.11"
  ipprotocol          = "inet"
  source_address      = "any"
  destination_address = "192.168.1.11"
  disabled            = true
  no_binat            = true
  nat_reflection      = "enable"
  description         = "Mail server 1:1 NAT (disabled)"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `description` (String) Description used as the unique identifier for this NAT 1:1 rule.
- `external` (String) External subnet IP for the 1:1 mapping. Enter the external (usually on a WAN) subnet for the 1:1 mapping (e.g. '10.0.0.1' or '10.0.0.0/24').
- `interface` (String) Network interface this 1:1 NAT rule applies to (e.g. 'wan', 'lan', 'opt1').

### Optional

- `destination_address` (String) Destination network. Can be 'any', a CIDR network, an alias, or a special pfSense interface address. Defaults to 'any'.
- `destination_not` (Boolean) Invert the destination address match.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable this 1:1 NAT rule.
- `ipprotocol` (String) IP protocol. Options: `'inet'`, `'inet6'`, `'inet46'`.
- `nat_reflection` (String) NAT reflection mode. Options: `''`, `'enable'`, `'disable'`, `'purenat'`. Empty string uses system default.
- `no_binat` (Boolean) Do not create a binat entry for this rule (one-way 1:1 NAT).
- `source_address` (String) Source network. Can be 'any', a CIDR network (e.g. '192.168.1.0/24'), an alias, or a special pfSense interface address. Defaults to 'any'.
- `source_not` (Boolean) Invert the source address match.

## Import

Import is supported using the rule description:

```shell
terraform import pfsense_firewall_nat_one_to_one.example "Web server 1:1 NAT"
```
